title: Reusable Launch Vehicles
shortSummary: The engineering of reusability — propulsive landing, refurbishment, flight-proven hardware, and how SpaceX made reusable rockets economically viable.
whyItMatters: Reusability is the single most important innovation in modern spaceflight. SpaceX has landed Falcon 9 boosters over 300 times. The economics are transformative: instead of throwing away a $60M booster after each flight, you refurbish and refly it. This is what makes space access affordable.
coreConcepts: Boostback, entry burn, and landing burn sequences
